name: King Zhou of Shang
shortDescription: The last king of the Shang Dynasty, remembered as a cruel tyrant in Chinese mythology and history. His reign is considered a period of decadence and oppression, ultimately leading to the dynasty's downfall.
description: King Zhou of Shang, also known as Di Xin, was the final ruler of the Shang Dynasty in ancient China. While historically a real figure, his image has been heavily mythologized and demonized over centuries. In mythology, he is depicted as a cruel, debauched, and tyrannical ruler who indulged in excessive pleasures and neglected his duties to the state. He is often portrayed as being easily influenced by his consort, Daji, a fox spirit in disguise, who further corrupted him and encouraged his wicked behavior. Stories depict him building lavish palaces, engaging in extravagant feasts, and inventing cruel forms of torture. His oppression of the people and disregard for the gods ultimately led to widespread rebellion and the rise of the Zhou Dynasty. He is considered a significant figure in Chinese mythology, embodying the archetype of the evil ruler whose actions bring about societal collapse. His story is often used as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked power and moral decay. His defeat by King Wu of Zhou is a central event in Chinese history and mythology, marking the beginning of a new era.
appearance: Often depicted as a physically imposing man, initially handsome but eventually becoming bloated and dissipated due to his excesses. He is frequently shown wearing extravagant robes and surrounded by symbols of his power and wealth, often with a cruel or disdainful expression.

sources.title: The Cambridge History of Ancient China
sources.author: Michael Loewe and Edward L. Shaughnessy
